subtasks.description: Query vendor reliability data from the data warehouse to retrieve vendor IDs, reliability metrics, incident counts, and historical performance indicators

Reconcile and classify vendor risk based on queried reliability data and incident history. Apply risk classification rules (low_risk/medium_risk/high_risk) based on thresholds derived from reliability metrics and incident frequency to produce vendor risk scores and classifications

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) sql_gen (mid-tier) to query vendor reliability data with multi-table joins of vendors, incidents, and performance metrics; (2) cross_recon (mid-tier) to apply classification logic and produce risk scores—cross_recon handles the reconciliation of raw metrics into risk classifications, which is moderate complexity requiring rule application and threshold evaluation. No policy_action needed; classification is part of the reconciliation logic, not a governance policy check. Total subtasks minimized to essential decomposition: data retrieval + risk classification.

output.sql: SELECT vendor_name AS vendor_id, COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'paid' THEN 1 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) AS reliability_metric, COUNT(CASE WHEN status != 'paid' THEN 1 END) AS incident_count, MIN(invoice_date) AS first_invoice_date, MAX(invoice_date) AS last_invoice_date, COUNT(DISTINCT invoice_id) AS total_invoices, CASE WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN status != 'paid' THEN 1 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) < 0.05 THEN 'low_risk' WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN status != 'paid' THEN 1 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) < 0.15 THEN 'medium_risk' ELSE 'high_risk' END AS risk_classification FROM invoice_facts GROUP BY vendor_name ORDER BY vendor_name
